Package microui.component

Class ToggleButton

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.base.ContentView
microui.core.base.Component
microui.core.AbstractButton
microui.component.ToggleButton
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
public final class ToggleButton extends AbstractButton
A toggle button component that can be switched between two states (active/inactive). The button visually indicates its state through a sliding thumb and a background color that changes based on the active state. Supports ripple effects, hover effects, and state change listeners.

  • Constructor Details

    • ToggleButton

      public ToggleButton(float x, float y, float w, float h)
      Constructs a toggle button with the specified position and size.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      w - the width of the button
      h - the height of the button

    • ToggleButton

      public ToggleButton()
      Constructs a toggle button with default size and positions it at the center of the sketch.

  • Method Details

    • addOnStateChangedListener

      public void addOnStateChangedListener(Listener listener)
      Adds a listener to be notified when the active state of the button changes.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to add (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if listener is null
      DuplicateItemException - if the listener has already been added

    • removeOnStateChangedListener

      public void removeOnStateChangedListener(Listener listener)
      Removes a previously added state change listener.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to remove (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if listener is null
      NoSuchElementException - if the listener is not found

    • getThumbColor

      public AbstractColor getThumbColor()
      Returns the current color of the sliding thumb.
      Returns:
      the thumb color (never null)

    • setThumbColor

      public void setThumbColor(AbstractColor thumbColor)
      Sets the color of the sliding thumb.
      Parameters:
      thumbColor - the new thumb color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if thumbColor is null

    • getActiveStateColor

      public AbstractColor getActiveStateColor()
      Returns the color used for the background when the button is in the active state.
      Returns:
      the active state color (never null)

    • setActiveStateColor

      public void setActiveStateColor(AbstractColor activeStateColor)
      Sets the color used for the background when the button becomes active.
      Parameters:
      activeStateColor - the new active state color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if activeStateColor is null

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Checks whether the button is currently in the active state.
      Returns:
      true if active, false otherwise

    • setActive

      public void setActive(boolean active)
      Sets the active state of the button. If the new state differs from the current one, all registered state change listeners are notified.
      Parameters:
      active - true to set active, false to set inactive

    • toggle

      public void toggle()
      Toggles the active state of the button. Equivalent to setActive(!isActive()).
